Astro Boy (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $19,551,067Details
International Box Office $22,085,176
Worldwide Box Office $41,636,243
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,309,300
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $604,375
Total $10,913,675
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In futuristic Metro City, a brilliant scientist named Tenma builds Astro Boy, a robotic child with superstrength, X-ray vision and the ability to fly. Astro Boy sets out to explore the world and find acceptance, learning what being human is all about in the process. Finding that his friends and family in Metro City are in danger, he uses his incredible powers to save all that he loves.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,702,923 (34.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$65,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,014 opening theaters/3,020 max. theaters, 3.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,018,978

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 23rd, 2009 (Wide) by Summit Entertainment
International Releases: January 31st, 2024 (Limited), released as Astro Boy (아톰 새로운 시작) (South Korea)
Video Release: March 16th, 2010 by Summit Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some action and peril, and brief mild language.
Running Time: 100 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Anime, Directing Yourself
Plot point: Inventor, Robot
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Imagi Studios, Summit Entertainment
Production Countries: United States, Hong Kong
Languages: English

Synopsis

In futuristic Metro City, a brilliant scientist named Tenma builds Astro Boy, a robotic child with superstrength, X-ray vision and the ability to fly. Astro Boy sets out to explore the world and find acceptance, learning what being human is all about in the process. Finding that his friends and family in Metro City are in danger, he uses his incredible powers to save all that he loves.

Opening Weekend:$6,702,923 (34.3% of total gross)
